title: fix(deps): update module golang.org/x/net to v0.36.0 [security]
body:

### GitHub Vulnerability Alerts

#### [CVE-2025-22870](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-22870)

Matching of hosts against proxy patterns can improperly treat an IPv6 zone ID as a hostname component. For example, when the NO_PROXY environment variable is set to "*.example.com", a request to "[::1%25.example.com]:80` will incorrectly match and not be proxied.

comments.body: [NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
Does [CVE-2025-22870](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-22870) affect podman? I couldn't find a direct code path even though podman vendors the affected piece of code.

@danishprakash I have no done a proper look but I think we are using the standard library for our http requests so in that case I don't think it would matter. Reading the description doesn't sounds like it is not an actual real issue for us. Bypassing a proxy when talking to link local addresses, I am not sure we can 0even use such addresses anywhere. DNS cannot resolve them and the direct ipv6 syntax for registry pushes/pulls is not support by c/image. Sounds totally unlikely that someone has a NO_PROXY that matches a link local zone ID that would work.

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Thanks for the explanation @Luap99, that helps.
